content:
# Final Review Planner Positions
**Producer:** agent:claude/claude-opus-4-6

## Summary

All three amendments identify genuine, code-verified defects in the timeout and retry infrastructure. Amendment fr-7-f5efd49b correctly identifies that reqwest's timeout semantics don't guarantee wall-clock enforcement for slow-streaming responses, making the `enforces_timeout() -> true` claim potentially misleading. Amendment fr-7-144385ef correctly identifies that TmuxAdapter delegates `enforces_timeout()` to ProcessBackendAdapter (returning true), but TmuxAdapter.invoke() never applies tokio::time::timeout — it polls in a loop with no timeout — so the 30-second buffer is added to the service timeout unnecessarily. Amendment fr-7-9872eaf7 correctly identifies that a missing OPENROUTER_API_KEY produces BackendUnavailable with failure_class: None, which maps to TransportFailure and triggers 5 retries for what is a deterministic configuration error.

## Votes

- `fr-7-f5efd49b`: Accept
  Verified: OpenRouterBackendAdapter returns enforces_timeout() -> true (line 359) and uses reqwest::Client::builder().timeout() (line 105). reqwest's timeout covers the entire request lifecycle but may not fire if the server trickle-streams data (bytes keep arriving, preventing the idle/total timeout from triggering). Unlike ProcessBackendAdapter which wraps in an explicit tokio::time::timeout (line 1045), OpenRouter has no wall-clock guarantee. This means the 30-second buffer added by the service (service.rs line 200-201) is granted under a false premise — the adapter may not actually fire first. The amendment correctly proposes either returning false or wrapping in tokio::time::timeout. This is a real behavioral gap, not theoretical.
- `fr-7-144385ef`: Accept
  Verified: TmuxAdapter.enforces_timeout() delegates to self.process.enforces_timeout() (tmux.rs line 453), which returns true (process_backend.rs line 1160). However, TmuxAdapter.invoke() (tmux.rs lines 471-570) never wraps anything in tokio::time::timeout — it calls wait_for_session_exit() which is an unbounded polling loop (tmux.rs lines 342-382). The process backend's own tokio::time::timeout (process_backend.rs line 1045) is never invoked because TmuxAdapter only calls build_command(), not the process adapter's invoke(). So TmuxAdapter advertises timeout enforcement it doesn't perform, causing the service to add a 30-second buffer (service.rs line 200) that delays cancellation without providing the expected adapter-level timeout. This is a concrete regression in timeout behavior for tmux mode.
- `fr-7-9872eaf7`: Accept
  Verified: api_key() (openrouter_backend.rs lines 64-76) returns AppError::BackendUnavailable with failure_class: None when OPENROUTER_API_KEY is missing. AppError::failure_class() (error.rs line 311) maps None to FailureClass::TransportFailure. RetryPolicy (retry_policy.rs line 45) retries TransportFailure 5 times. A missing API key is a deterministic prerequisite failure that will never succeed on retry — burning 5 attempts wastes time and obscures the real error. The fix is straightforward: set failure_class to Some(FailureClass::BinaryNotFound) or a similar terminal class, or add a dedicated prerequisite failure class. This is a real usability and correctness bug.
